- Testid on theme headers: `data-testid="theme-group-{THEME_NAME}"`
  - Example: `data-testid="theme-group-COST"` for cost fields grouped header

---

### 5. **Navigation Header**

**Component**: `/web/src/components/layout/Header.tsx`

**Layout**: Sticky header, full width, dark blue background (`var(--color-primary)`)

**Desktop Structure** (visible from `md` breakpoint and above):
- **Logo/Wordmark** (clickable link to home):
  - `<Link to="/" />`
  - Icon: `<GraduationCap size={28} />` (accent gold color)
  - Text: "College Power Search" (white, semi-bold)
  - Font size: lg
  
- **Desktop Nav Links** (visibleFrom="md"):
  - Home: `<NavLink to="/" />`
  - About: `<NavLink to="/about" />`
  - Active link styling: accent gold color
  - Inactive styling: white with 80% opacity

- **Desktop Auth Actions** (visibleFrom="md"):
  - If loading: nothing rendered
  - If user logged in: `<UserMenu />`
  - If user not logged in: `<SignInMenu />`

**Mobile Structure** (hiddenFrom="md"):
- **Burger menu**: `<Burger opened={drawerOpened} onClick={toggleDrawer} aria-label="Toggle navigation" />`
  - Color: white
  - Selector for mobile: `button[aria-label="Toggle navigation"]`
  
- **Mobile Drawer** (Mantine Drawer component):
  - Title: "Navigation"
  - Size: "xs"
  - Contains:
    - Home and About NavLinks (stacked)
    - Auth component (SignInMenu or UserMenu)
  - Close on link click
  - Selector: `[role="dialog"]` for the drawer itself

---

### 6. **Authentication UI**

**Sign In Component**: `/web/src/components/auth/SignInMenu.tsx`

**Sign In Button**:
- Visible when user is not logged in
- Button text: "Sign In"
- Variant: "accent-outline" (gold/accent color outline)
- Size: "sm"
- Selector: `button:has-text("Sign In")`

**Sign In Menu Dropdown** (Mantine Menu):
- Opens on button click
- Single option: "Continue with Google"
- Clicking triggers: `supabase.auth.signInWithOAuth({ provider: 'google', ... })`
- Redirect to: `/auth/callback`

---

**User Menu Component**: `/web/src/components/auth/UserMenu.tsx`

**User Avatar Trigger**:
- Avatar with user initials or image
- `src={avatarUrl}` from Supabase user metadata
- Size: "sm", color: "accent"
- Cursor: pointer
- Selector: Avatar is in a Group with gap="xs"

**User Menu Dropdown**:
- **Header label**: 
  - Full name (from `user_metadata.full_name`)
  - Email (from `user.email`)
  - Both truncated if too long
  
- **Menu divider**
  
- **Sign out item**:
  - Text: "Sign out"
  - Color: red
  - Selector: `[role="menuitem"]:has-text("Sign out")`
  - Handler: `signOut()` from authStore

---

### 7. **Home Page Sample Searches**

**Component**: `/web/src/components/search/SampleSearchCarousel.tsx`

**Carousel Section**:
- Title: "Explore Sample Searches" (centered, heading level 2)
- Uses Mantine Carousel with:
  - Loop enabled
  - With controls (prev/next buttons)
  - Responsive slide sizes: 100% (mobile), 50% (tablet), 33% (desktop)
  - Slide gap: "lg"

**Sample Search Cards**:
- Testid: `data-testid="sample-search-card"`
- Component: `<Card component={Link} />`
- Contents:
  - Icon box (44×44px, brand blue background)
  - Title text (large, brand blue)
  - Description text (small, dimmed)
  - List of up to 4 sample institutions from that search:
    - Format: "Institution Name — City, State"
    - Or skeleton loading bars while fetching
  - Result count link (accent gold): "{total.toLocaleString()} results ›"
  
- **Click behavior**: Navigates to `/search?{filters}` URL

**Selector for first card**: `[data-testid="sample-search-card"]:first-of-type`

---

### 8. **Results View Modes**

**View Toggle Component**: `/web/src/components/search/ViewToggle.tsx`

**Control**:
- Mantine `SegmentedControl` with 4 options
- Size: "xs"
- Aria-label: "View mode"
- Selector: `[aria-label="View mode"]`

**View Modes**:
1. **Card** (default): `value="card"`
   - Icon: `<LayoutGrid size={16} />`
   - Input: `<input value="card" />`
   - Trigger: `input[value="card"] + label` or segmented control click
   
2. **List**: `value="list"`
   - Icon: `<List size={16} />`
   - Selector: `input[value="list"] + label`
   
3. **Table**: `value="table"`
   - Icon: `<Table2 size={16} />`
   - Selector: `input[value="table"] + label`
   - Shows column picker button when active
   
4. **Map**: `value="map"`
   - Icon: `<Map size={16} />`
   - Selector: `input[value="map"] + label`

**State Persistence**:
- Saved to localStorage: `kapow:results-view`
- Also persisted to URL as `?view=` param

---

### 9. **Results List & Cards**

**Institution Card** (`InstitutionCard.tsx`):
- Clickable card with hover state
- Contains:
  - Institution name (brand blue, semi-bold)
  - City, state
  - **Distance** (if `hasLocation`): `data-testid="distance"` shows formatted distance
  - **My Price** (if `hasState`): `data-testid="my-price"` shows formatted price
  - Additional metadata fields

**Loading Skeleton** (`InstitutionCardSkeleton`):
- Testid: `data-testid="result-skeleton"`
- Shown while results are loading

**Results Loading Container**:
- Testid: `data-testid="results-loading"`
- Contains multiple skeletons while fetching

---

### 10. **Results Table Specifics**

**Table Structure** (`ResultsTable.tsx`):
- Mantine `Table` component with `striped` and `highlightOnHover`
- **Two header rows** (when extra fields visible):
  1. Theme group spanning headers (COST, ACADEMICS, OUTCOMES, etc.)
     - Testid: `data-testid="theme-group-{THEME}"`
  2. Column headers with sort controls

**Header Cells**:
- Sortable headers: `<UnstyledButton>` with `data-testid="sort-{fieldName}"`
  - Example: `data-testid="sort-name"`, `data-testid="sort-distance"`, `data-testid="sort-city"`
  - Click to toggle ASC → DESC → clear
  - Sort icon: `<ArrowUp size={14} />` (ASC) or `<ArrowDown size={14} />` (DESC) or `<ArrowUpDown size={14} opacity="0.3" />` (inactive)

**Data Cells**:
- Name column: sticky left, brand blue, semi-bold
- Distance: `data-testid="distance"` (format: "45 mi", "< 1 mi", "—")
- My Price: `data-testid="my-price"` (format: "$45,000")
- Blend Score: `data-testid="blend-score"` (format: "75.3" representing percentage)

**Table Rows**:
- Clickable: `onClick={() => onClickInstitution(unitID)}`
- Cursor: pointer
- Hover: highlight color

---

### 11. **Detail Modal**

**Component**: `/web/src/components/search/InstitutionDetail.tsx`

**Modal Trigger**: Click on any institution card/row

**Modal Properties**:
- Mantine `Modal` component
- `opened={detailOpened}`
- `onClose={() => setDetailOpened(false)}`
- Full-width on mobile, responsive on desktop

**Content Structure**:
- Institution name, city, state heading
- Detail rows with labels and values:
  - Type (Public/Private nonprofit/Private for-profit)
  - Enrollment
  - Graduation rate
  - Admission rate
  - In-state tuition
  - Out-of-state tuition
  - Average net price
  - Retention rate
  - Student-faculty ratio
  
- Divider
- Optional "Similar Institutions" section (expandable)

---

### 12. **Pagination Controls**

**Location**: Bottom of results section (only if `totalPages > 1`)

**Testid**: `data-testid="pagination-controls"`

**Components**:
- **Pagination**: Mantine `Pagination` component
  - Total pages
  - Current page number buttons
  - Click to go to page N
  - Selector: `button:has-text("{pageNumber}")`
  
- **Page size selector**: `<NativeSelect>`
  - ID: "page-size"
  - Aria-label: "Per page"
  - Options: 5, 10, 20, 50, 100
  - Default: 20
  - Selector: `select#page-size` or `[aria-label="Per page"]`

---

## Summary Table: Key Selectors by Feature

| Feature | Primary Selector | Testid | Notes |
|---------|-----------------|--------|-------|
| Ranking blend builder | Paper container | `ranking-blend-builder` | Sliders per field, reset button |
| Export CSV | Button text "Export CSV" | None (use text) | Downloads to `/api/export/csv?...` |
| Export PDF | Button text "Export PDF" | None (use text) | Opens download dialog |
| Location input ZIP | `input[label="ZIP code"]` | None (use label) | 5-digit validation |
| Geolocation button | Button "Use my location" | None (use text) | Browser geolocation |
| Location display | MapPin icon + text | None | Editable via LocationInput |
| Column picker | Button "Columns" | None (use text) | Popover with checkboxes & drag |
| Column order item | — | `column-order-item-{fieldName}` | Draggable with up/down arrows |
| Header logo | Link to "/" | None | GraduationCap icon + "College Power Search" |
| Header nav Home | NavLink to "/" | None (use text) | Active styling when on home |
| Header nav About | NavLink to "/about" | None (use text) | Active styling when on /about |
| Mobile burger | Button | `aria-label="Toggle navigation"` | Toggles mobile drawer |
| Mobile drawer | Dialog | `role="dialog"` | Contains nav links + auth |
| Sign in button | Button "Sign In" | None (use text) | Opens Google OAuth flow |
| User avatar | Avatar | None | Shows initials or image |
| User menu sign out | Menu item "Sign out" | None (use text) | Red text, triggers logout |
| Sample search card | Card | `sample-search-card` | Carousel items, clickable links |
| View toggle | SegmentedControl | `aria-label="View mode"` | 4 icons: card, list, table, map |
| View selector | Input radio | `value="card/list/table/map"` | Click label to switch |
| Institution card distance | Distance text | `distance` | Only if `hasLocation` |
| Institution card price | Price text | `my-price` | Only if `hasState` |
| Card loading skeleton | Skeleton | `result-skeleton` | Multiple while loading |
| Results loading area | Container | `results-loading` | Contains skeletons |
| Sort header | Button/link | `sort-{fieldName}` | Toggles ASC/DESC/clear |
| Table distance cell | Table cell | `distance` | Per-row distance value |
| Table price cell | Table cell | `my-price` | Per-row price value |
| Blend score cell | Table cell | `blend-score` | When ranking blend active |
| Theme group header | Table header | `theme-group-{THEME}` | Spanning headers (COST, ACADEMIC, etc.) |
| Pagination | Controls | `pagination-controls` | Page buttons + per-page select |
| Per page select | NativeSelect | `id="page-size"` | Options: 5, 10, 20, 50, 100 |

---

This covers the main UI areas relevant to E2E tests for ranking blend, export, geographic search, table customization, navigation, and auth.

## Task: Write E2E tests for kapow-25l (Ranking Blend & Export)

Create `web/e2e/blend-export.spec.ts` with Playwright E2E tests covering ranking blend sliders and CSV/PDF export.

### Issue Description
End-to-end tests for ranking blend sliders and CSV/PDF export. Tags: `@blend`, `@export`.

**Tests needed:**

### Critical (~1 test)
- Adjust one ranking blend slider → results reorder by blend score

### Standard (~3 tests)
- Reset blend → returns to default sorting
- Click 'Export CSV' → file downloads successfully
- Click 'Export PDF' → file downloads successfully

### Comprehensive (~3 tests)
- Blend weights reflected in URL query params
- Export respects active filters (exported data matches filtered results, not all institutions)
- Multiple blend sliders combine correctly (weights shown as percentages)

### Existing patterns to follow

Import from helpers:
```ts
import { test, expect } from '@playwright/test'
import { searchWith, waitForResults, getResultCount } from './helpers'
```

`searchWith(page, { key: 'value' })` navigates to `/search?key=value`.
`waitForResults(page)` waits for loading skeleton to disappear and results text to appear.
`getResultCount(page)` parses the result count number.

### Tag convention
Each test title ends with tier + feature tags:
- `@critical @blend`, `@standard @export`, `@comprehensive @blend`, etc.

### Key UI selectors (from actual component code)

**Ranking Blend Builder** (`data-testid="ranking-blend-builder"`):
- Each ranking field has `data-field="{fieldName}"` attribute on its container Box
- Fields: `rank_us_news_national`, `rank_us_news_liberal_arts`, `rank_forbes`, `rank_washington_monthly_national`, `rank_washington_monthly_liberal_arts`, `rank_niche`, `score_money`
- Each field has a Mantine Slider with `aria-label="{label} weight"` (e.g., `aria-label="US News National weight"`)
- Weight display shows value and percentage like "45 (30%)"
- Reset button: `button:has-text("Reset")` — only visible when totalWeight > 0
- Blend state serialized to URL as `?blend=rank_us_news_national:50,rank_forbes:30`

**Export buttons** (in results toolbar):
- CSV: `page.getByRole('button', { name: /Export CSV/i })` — disabled when 0 results
- PDF: `page.getByRole('button', { name: /Export PDF/i })` — disabled when 0 results
- CSV export opens `/api/export/csv?filters=...&sort=...` (may open as popup or trigger download)
- PDF export triggers a file download via `downloadSearchPdf()` helper

**Important**: The slider `.fill('50')` approach may not work with Mantine sliders. Instead, use keyboard-based interaction or direct input. Try:
```ts
const slider = blendBuilder.getByRole('slider', { name: 'US News National weight' })
await slider.fill('50')
```
If that doesn't work, use `slider.press('ArrowRight')` repeatedly or set via URL params.

### Quality gate
After writing the spec file, run:
```bash
cd web && pnpm build && pnpm lint
```
This must pass with zero errors/warnings. (Don't run E2E tests — they need a running server.)

### Git
Commit with message format:
```
feat: add E2E tests for ranking blend and export
```

```
Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
```

## Task: Write E2E tests for kapow-7at (Geographic Features)

Create `web/e2e/geo-features.spec.ts` with Playwright E2E tests covering ZIP code resolution, distance display, and sample searches.

### Issue Description
End-to-end tests for ZIP code resolution, distance display, and sample searches. Tag: `@geo`.

**Tests needed:**

### Critical (~1 test)
- Enter valid ZIP code → location resolves, distance appears on result cards

### Standard (~3 tests)
- 'Sort by distance' button appears when location is set
- Clear location → distance display disappears from results
- Click sample search card on home page → navigates to /search with correct filters applied

### Comprehensive (~2 tests)
- Invalid ZIP code handled gracefully (no crash, appropriate feedback)
- Distance values are reasonable numbers (not NaN, null, or negative)

### Existing patterns to follow

Import from helpers:
```ts
import { test, expect } from '@playwright/test'
import { searchWith, waitForResults, getResultCount } from './helpers'
```

`searchWith(page, { key: 'value' })` navigates to `/search?key=value`.
`waitForResults(page)` waits for loading skeleton to disappear and results text to appear.
`getResultCount(page)` parses the result count number.

### Tag convention
Each test title ends with tier + feature tags: `@critical @geo`, `@standard @geo`, `@comprehensive @geo`

### Key UI selectors (from actual component code)

**Location Input** (`LocationInput.tsx`):
- ZIP input: `page.getByLabel('ZIP code')` — accepts 5-digit format, placeholder "e.g. 02134"
- Go button: `page.getByRole('button', { name: 'Go' })` — validates ZIP and triggers resolution
- "Use my location" button: `page.getByRole('button', { name: 'Use my location' })`
- Clear button: `page.getByRole('button', { name: 'Clear location' })` (or `aria-label="Clear location"`)
- When location is set, displays "City, State" or lat/long text with a MapPin icon

**GraphQL**: `ResolveZip($zip: String!)` returns `{ zip, latitude, longitude, state, city }`

**Distance display in results**:
- Institution cards: `data-testid="distance"` — shows "45 mi", "< 1 mi", or "—"
- Results table: `data-testid="distance"` on each cell
- Sort by distance header: `data-testid="sort-distance"` (only useful when location is set)

**URL params when location set**: `?userLat=42.33&userLng=-71.11&userState=MA`

**Sample Search Cards** (`SampleSearchCarousel.tsx`):
- Cards have `data-testid="sample-search-card"`
- Each is a `<Card component={Link}>` — click navigates to `/search?{filters}`
- Contains title text, description, sample institutions, and "{N} results ›" link
- Carousel on the home page under heading "Explore Sample Searches"

**Important notes**:
- The ZIP resolution is async (GraphQL query). After filling ZIP and clicking Go, wait for the location display to appear.
- Distance display only appears on cards when `hasLocation` is true in the search state.
- For the "Sort by distance" test, navigate to table view with a location set and check for `sort-distance` testid.

### Quality gate
After writing the spec file, run:
```bash
cd web && pnpm build && pnpm lint
```
This must pass with zero errors/warnings.

### Git
Commit with message format:
```
feat: add E2E tests for geographic features
```

```
Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
```

## Task: Write E2E tests for kapow-ce0 (Table Column Customization)

Create `web/e2e/table-columns.spec.ts` with Playwright E2E tests for the table view column picker.

### Issue Description
End-to-end tests for the table view column picker. Tag: `@columns`.

**Tests needed:**

### Critical (~1 test)
- Table view shows expected default columns

### Standard (~2 tests)
- Open column picker → uncheck a column → column header disappears from table
- Re-check the column → column reappears

### Comprehensive (~2 tests)
- Column visibility persists across page reload (localStorage `kapow:visible-columns`)
- Column order changes via picker are reflected in table header order

### Existing patterns to follow

Import from helpers:
```ts
import { test, expect } from '@playwright/test'
import { searchWith, waitForResults } from './helpers'
```

To switch to table view:
```ts
await page.locator('input[value="table"] + label').click()
```

### Tag convention
Each test title ends with tier + feature tags: `@critical @columns`, `@standard @columns`, `@comprehensive @columns`

### Key UI selectors (from actual component code)

**View Toggle**: SegmentedControl with `input[value="table"] + label` to click table view

**Column Picker** (`ColumnPicker.tsx`):
- Trigger button: `page.getByRole('button', { name: 'Columns' })` — only visible in table view
- Opens a Mantine Popover dropdown (width 300px, max-height 500px scrollable)

**Inside the popover:**
- Checkboxes grouped by theme headings (CORE, LOCATION, ACADEMICS, COST, OUTCOMES, RANKINGS)
- Each checkbox has a label with the column display name (e.g., "City", "State", "Enrollment", etc.)
- The "Name" checkbox is always checked and disabled (pinned column)
- Use: `popover.getByRole('checkbox', { name: 'City' })` to find a checkbox
- Check/uncheck: `checkbox.click()` or `checkbox.check()` / `checkbox.uncheck()`

**Column Order section** (below checkboxes, after divider):
- Heading: "Column Order"
- Each reorderable item: `data-testid="column-order-item-{fieldName}"` (e.g., `column-order-item-city`)
- Up arrow button: `aria-label="Move {label} up"`
- Down arrow button: `aria-label="Move {label} down"`

**Table headers**:
- The results table `<table>` has `<th>` elements
- Sortable headers have `data-testid="sort-{fieldName}"` (e.g., `sort-name`, `sort-city`)
- Theme group spanning headers: `data-testid="theme-group-{THEME}"` (e.g., `theme-group-COST`)

**localStorage keys**:
- `kapow:visible-columns` — JSON array of visible column field names
- `kapow:column-order` — JSON array of column order

**Default visible columns**: The default set includes Name (always), City, State, and a few others. Check what's visible by default in table view without any localStorage set.

**Important notes**:
- First navigate to `/search` and wait for results, then switch to table view
- The column picker popover closes when clicking outside — keep interactions within it
- After toggling a column, the table re-renders; wait for the table to be visible
- For column order test, look at the actual `<th>` elements' text content order in the table

### Quality gate
After writing the spec file, run:
```bash
cd web && pnpm build && pnpm lint
```
This must pass with zero errors/warnings.

### Git
Commit with message format:
```
feat: add E2E tests for table column customization
```

```
Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
```

## Task: Write E2E tests for kapow-9iu (Navigation, Deep Links & Responsive)

Create `web/e2e/nav-responsive.spec.ts` with Playwright E2E tests for navigation, URL deep linking, and responsive layouts.

### Issue Description
End-to-end tests for navigation, URL deep linking, and responsive layouts. Tags: `@nav`, `@responsive`.

**Tests needed:**

### Critical (~1 test)
- Navigate directly to `/search?state=CA` → state filter pre-populated, matching results shown

### Standard (~3 tests)
- Header nav: Home → About → Home works via nav links
- Logo click returns to home from any page
- Mobile viewport (375px): hamburger menu appears, desktop nav hidden

### Comprehensive (~3 tests)
- Mobile: filter collapse 'Show/Hide Filters' toggle works on search page
- Tablet (768px): layout adapts correctly (collapsible filters above results)
- All filters round-trip through URL: set filters → copy URL → navigate to URL → same filter state and results

### Existing patterns to follow

Import from helpers:
```ts
import { test, expect } from '@playwright/test'
import { searchWith, waitForResults, getResultCount } from './helpers'
```

### Tag convention
Each test title ends with tier + feature tags: `@critical @nav`, `@standard @nav`, `@standard @responsive`, `@comprehensive @responsive`, etc.

### Key UI selectors (from actual component code)

**Header** (`Header.tsx`):
- Logo link: `<a>` or `<Link>` wrapping GraduationCap icon + "College Power Search" text, links to "/"
  - Find via: `page.getByRole('link', { name: /College Power Search/i })` or `page.locator('a[href="/"]').filter({ hasText: 'College Power Search' })`
- Desktop nav links (visible from `md` breakpoint = 768px+):
  - Home: `page.getByRole('link', { name: 'Home' })` (NavLink to "/")
  - About: `page.getByRole('link', { name: 'About' })` (NavLink to "/about")
- Mobile burger: `page.getByRole('button', { name: 'Toggle navigation' })` or `button[aria-label="Toggle navigation"]`
  - Only visible below `md` breakpoint (< 768px)
- Mobile drawer: Mantine Drawer with title "Navigation", contains Home + About links

**Important Mantine breakpoints**:
- `md` = 768px — below this, desktop nav hides and burger appears
- Default viewport in Playwright is 1280x720, so desktop nav is visible by default

**Deep linking / URL state**:
- Filters serialize to URL: `?state=CA`, `?q=MIT`, `?enrollment=1000,5000`, etc.
- State filter in sidebar: `page.locator('label', { has: page.getByText('State') }).locator('select')` — check its value
- Name filter: `page.getByRole('textbox', { name: 'Name' })`

**Filter sidebar on mobile**:
- On mobile, the filter sidebar collapses. Look for a "Show Filters" / "Hide Filters" toggle button.
- Or it may use a "Filters" button that opens a drawer/collapse panel.
- Use `page.getByRole('button', { name: /Filters|Show Filters|Hide Filters/i })` to find it.

**About page**: Route is `/about` — verify it has heading content

### Responsive testing approach
Use `page.setViewportSize({ width: 375, height: 812 })` for mobile and `page.setViewportSize({ width: 768, height: 1024 })` for tablet. Reset to default for desktop tests if needed.

### Quality gate
After writing the spec file, run:
```bash
cd web && pnpm build && pnpm lint
```
This must pass with zero errors/warnings.

### Git
Commit with message format:
```
feat: add E2E tests for navigation, deep links, and responsive layouts

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
```

input.prompt:
## Task: Write E2E tests for kapow-xll (Authentication & Preferences)

Create `web/e2e/auth-prefs.spec.ts` with Playwright E2E tests for authentication UI states and user preferences.

### Issue Description
End-to-end tests for authentication UI states and user preferences. Tag: `@auth`.

Requires either a test Supabase account or mock auth setup for signed-in state tests.

**Tests needed:**

### Critical (~1 test)
- Unauthenticated state: 'Sign In' button visible in header

### Standard (~2 tests)
- Sign-in dropdown shows 'Continue with Google' option
- (If mock auth available) Signed-in state shows user menu with name/email

### Comprehensive (~1 test)
- Preferences round-trip: save home location → reload page → location persists

### Notes from issue
- Full OAuth flow testing requires either a test account or mock Supabase setup
- Consider using Playwright's route interception to mock auth responses

### Existing patterns to follow

Import from helpers:
```ts
import { test, expect } from '@playwright/test'
import { searchWith, waitForResults } from './helpers'
```

### Tag convention
Each test title ends with tier + feature tags: `@critical @auth`, `@standard @auth`, `@comprehensive @auth`

### Key UI selectors (from actual component code)

**Sign In Menu** (`SignInMenu.tsx`):
- Sign In button: `page.getByRole('button', { name: 'Sign In' })` — visible when user is NOT logged in
- Variant: "accent-outline" (gold border)
- Clicking opens a Mantine Menu dropdown
- Menu item: "Continue with Google" — triggers `supabase.auth.signInWithOAuth({ provider: 'google' })`

**User Menu** (`UserMenu.tsx`) — visible when user IS logged in:
- Avatar element showing user initials or image
- Click opens menu with:
  - User's full name (from `user_metadata.full_name`)
  - User's email
  - Divider
  - "Sign out" menu item (red color)

**Header layout**:
- Desktop (≥768px): Auth buttons in header right side
- Mobile (<768px): Auth components in mobile drawer

**Location Preferences** (for preferences round-trip test):
- Location is stored in localStorage or URL params
- The LocationInput component allows setting ZIP code
- ZIP input: `page.getByLabel('ZIP code')` with "Go" button
- Once set, location appears as "City, State" text
- URL gets `?userLat=...&userLng=...&userState=...`
- On reload, if URL params are present, location is restored

**Important implementation notes**:
1. For the unauthenticated tests, no mock setup needed — just verify the Sign In button and dropdown
2. For the signed-in test, use Playwright's route interception to mock the Supabase auth state. The app reads auth from a Zustand store (`authStore`) that hydrates from Supabase. You can either:
   - Mock the Supabase auth endpoint responses
   - Or skip this test with `test.skip()` and a comment explaining it needs mock auth setup
   - **Recommended**: Skip the signed-in test for now with a clear comment, since mocking Supabase auth state is complex and brittle
3. For preferences round-trip, use ZIP code entry (which triggers a GraphQL query), then reload and verify. Since this needs a running server, the test will work when E2E tests run against the dev server.

### Quality gate
After writing the spec file, run:
```bash
cd web && pnpm build && pnpm lint
```
This must pass with zero errors/warnings.

### Git
Commit with message format:
```
feat: add E2E tests for authentication UI and preferences

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
```

**File: `web/e2e/geo-features.spec.ts`**

```typescript
import { test, expect } from '@playwright/test'
import { searchWith, waitForResults } from './helpers'

// ── Critical ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

test.describe('Geographic Features — Critical', () => {
  test('enter valid ZIP code resolves location and distance appears on result cards @critical @geo', async ({
    page,
  }) => {
    // Navigate to search page first so the LocationInput is visible
    await searchWith(page, {})
    await waitForResults(page)

    // Fill in a known ZIP code and submit
    const zipInput = page.getByLabel('ZIP code')
    await zipInput.fill('02134')
    await page.getByRole('button', { name: 'Go' }).click()

    // Wait for the location to resolve — the ZIP input disappears and is
    // replaced by a display showing "City, State" text with a MapPin icon.
    // Once resolved, the Clear location button becomes visible.
    await expect(page.getByLabel('Clear location')).toBeVisible({ timeout: 15_000 })

    // URL should now contain userLat and userLng params
    await expect(page).toHaveURL(/userLat=/)
    await expect(page).toHaveURL(/userLng=/)

    // Wait for results to reload with distance data
    await waitForResults(page)

    // Distance badges should appear on the institution cards
    const distanceBadges = page.getByTestId('distance')
    await expect(distanceBadges.first()).toBeVisible({ timeout: 10_000 })

    // Verify the distance text matches expected format: "< 1 mi" or "N mi"
    const distanceText = await distanceBadges.first().textContent()
    expect(distanceText).toMatch(/^\s*(<\s*1\s*mi|\d[\d,]*\s*mi|\u2014)\s*$/)
  })
})

// ── Standard ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

test.describe('Geographic Features — Standard', () => {
  test('sort by distance button appears when location is set @standard @geo', async ({
    page,
  }) => {
    // Navigate with location already set via URL params (Boston area)
    await searchWith(page, {
      userLat: '42.33',
      userLng: '-71.11',
      userState: 'MA',
    })
    await waitForResults(page)

    // The "Sort by distance" button should be visible
    const sortButton = page.getByRole('button', { name: /sort by distance/i })
    await expect(sortButton).toBeVisible()
  })

  test('clear location removes distance display from results @standard @geo', async ({
    page,
  }) => {
    // Start with location set via URL params
    await searchWith(page, {
      userLat: '42.33',
      userLng: '-71.11',
      userState: 'MA',
    })
    await waitForResults(page)

    // Distance badges should be visible initially
    const distanceBadges = page.getByTestId('distance')
    await expect(distanceBadges.first()).toBeVisible({ timeout: 10_000 })

    // Clear the location
    await page.getByLabel('Clear location').click()

    // Wait for results to reload without distance data
    await waitForResults(page)

    // Distance badges should no longer be visible
    await expect(distanceBadges).toHaveCount(0)

    // URL should no longer contain location params
    const url = page.url()
    expect(url).not.toContain('userLat=')
    expect(url).not.toContain('userLng=')

    // The "Sort by distance" button should also disappear
    await expect(
      page.getByRole('button', { name: /sort by distance/i }),
    ).toBeHidden()
  })

  test('click sample search card on home page navigates to /search with correct filters @standard @geo', async ({
    page,
  }) => {
    await page.goto('/')

    // Wait for sample search cards to appear
    const sampleCards = page.getByTestId('sample-search-card')
    await expect(sampleCards.first()).toBeVisible({ timeout: 15_000 })

    // Get the href from the first sample search card (it's a Link component)
    const firstCard = sampleCards.first()
    const href = await firstCard.getAttribute('href')
    expect(href).toBeTruthy()
    expect(href).toContain('/search?')

    // Click the first sample search card
    await firstCard.click()

    // Should navigate to the search page with filter params from the card
    await expect(page).toHaveURL(/\/search\?/)

    // Results should load successfully
    await waitForResults(page)
  })
})

// ── Comprehensive ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

test.describe('Geographic Features — Comprehensive', () => {
  test('invalid ZIP code handled gracefully without crash @comprehensive @geo', async ({
    page,
  }) => {
    await searchWith(page, {})
    await waitForResults(page)

    // Try a non-5-digit input
    const zipInput = page.getByLabel('ZIP code')
    await zipInput.fill('abc')
    await page.getByRole('button', { name: 'Go' }).click()

    // Should show an error message about the format
    await expect(page.getByText('Enter a 5-digit ZIP code')).toBeVisible()

    // The page should not crash — results should still be visible
    await expect(
      page.getByText(/institutions found|results/i),
    ).toBeVisible()

    // Try a valid-format ZIP that does not exist (unlikely to be in the DB)
    await zipInput.fill('00000')
    await page.getByRole('button', { name: 'Go' }).click()

    // Should show "ZIP code not found" or similar error, not crash
    // Wait a moment for the query to complete
    await page.waitForTimeout(2000)
    await page.getByRole('button', { name: 'Go' }).click()
    await expect(
      page.getByText(/ZIP code not found|Looking up ZIP/),
    ).toBeVisible({ timeout: 10_000 })

    // Page should still be functional
    await expect(
      page.getByText(/institutions found|results/i),
    ).toBeVisible()
  })

  test('distance values are reasonable numbers when location is set @comprehensive @geo', async ({
    page,
  }) => {
    // Navigate to search in table view with a location set (Boston area)
    await searchWith(page, {
      userLat: '42.33',
      userLng: '-71.11',
      userState: 'MA',
      view: 'table',
    })
    await waitForResults(page)

    // Table should be visible since we requested table view
    await expect(page.locator('table')).toBeVisible({ timeout: 10_000 })

    // Get all distance cells
    const distanceCells = page.getByTestId('distance')
    const cellCount = await distanceCells.count()
    expect(cellCount).toBeGreaterThan(0)

    // Check each distance value is a reasonable number
    for (let i = 0; i < Math.min(cellCount, 10); i++) {
      const text = await distanceCells.nth(i).textContent()
      expect(text).toBeTruthy()

      // Should match "< 1 mi", "N mi", "N,NNN mi", or the em dash for null
      expect(text!.trim()).toMatch(/^(<\s*1\s*mi|\d[\d,]*\s*mi|\u2014)$/)

      // If it's a numeric distance, verify it's a reasonable value (0-15000 miles)
      const numMatch = text!.match(/^(\d[\d,]*)\s*mi$/)
      if (numMatch) {
        const miles = Number(numMatch[1].replace(/,/g, ''))
        expect(miles).toBeGreaterThanOrEqual(0)
        expect(miles).toBeLessThan(15_000) // Earth circumference / 2 ~ 12,400 mi
      }
    }
  })
})
```

### Issue Description
End-to-end tests for ranking blend sliders and CSV/PDF export. Tags: `@blend`, `@export`.

**Tests needed:**

### Critical (~1 test)
- Adjust one ranking blend slider → results reorder by blend score

### Standard (~3 tests)
- Reset blend → returns to default sorting
- Click 'Export CSV' → file downloads successfully
- Click 'Export PDF' → file downloads successfully

### Comprehensive (~3 tests)
- Blend weights reflected in URL query params
- Export respects active filters (exported data matches filtered results, not all institutions)
- Multiple blend sliders combine correctly (weights shown as percentages)

### Existing patterns to follow

Import from helpers:
```ts
import { test, expect } from '@playwright/test'
import { searchWith, waitForResults, getResultCount } from './helpers'
```

`searchWith(page, { key: 'value' })` navigates to `/search?key=value`.
`waitForResults(page)` waits for loading skeleton to disappear and results text to appear.
`getResultCount(page)` parses the result count number.

### Tag convention
Each test title ends with tier + feature tags:
- `@critical @blend`, `@standard @export`, `@comprehensive @blend`, etc.

### Key UI selectors (from actual component code)

**Ranking Blend Builder** (`data-testid="ranking-blend-builder"`):
- Each ranking field has `data-field="{fieldName}"` attribute on its container Box
- Fields: `rank_us_news_national`, `rank_us_news_liberal_arts`, `rank_forbes`, `rank_washington_monthly_national`, `rank_washington_monthly_liberal_arts`, `rank_niche`, `score_money`
- Each field has a Mantine Slider with `aria-label="{label} weight"` (e.g., `aria-label="US News National weight"`)
- Weight display shows value and percentage like "45 (30%)"
- Reset button: `button:has-text("Reset")` — only visible when totalWeight > 0
- Blend state serialized to URL as `?blend=rank_us_news_national:50,rank_forbes:30`

**Export buttons** (in results toolbar):
- CSV: `page.getByRole('button', { name: /Export CSV/i })` — disabled when 0 results
- PDF: `page.getByRole('button', { name: /Export PDF/i })` — disabled when 0 results
- CSV export opens `/api/export/csv?filters=...&sort=...` (may open as popup or trigger download)
- PDF export triggers a file download via `downloadSearchPdf()` helper

**Important**: The slider `.fill('50')` approach may not work with Mantine sliders. Instead, use keyboard-based interaction or direct input. Try:
```ts
const slider = blendBuilder.getByRole('slider', { name: 'US News National weight' })
await slider.fill('50')
```
If that doesn't work, use `slider.press('ArrowRight')` repeatedly or set via URL params.

```bash
cd web && pnpm build && pnpm lint
```
This must pass with zero errors/warnings. (Don't run E2E tests — they need a running server.)

Commit with message format:

```
feat: add E2E tests for ranking blend and export

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
```

{"parentUuid":"b78f05ed-27ee-4e8b-834e-7a6b3b73e1b5","isSidechain":true,"userType":"external","cwd":"/home/ketan/project/kapow/.claude/worktrees/agent-a6b26db5","sessionId":"671e5b9c-c068-4bab-b9b5-75da76d0514f","version":"2.1.71","gitBranch":"worktree-agent-a6b26db5","agentId":"a6b26db53ec6af3df","slug":"golden-plotting-crane","type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01NLtcLPYpt4YQcLzce6C76o","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→import { type Page, expect } from '@playwright/test'
     2→
     3→/**
     4→ * Shared E2E test utilities for Kapow.
     5→ *
     6→ * Tagging convention for test titles:
     7→ *
     8→ *   Tiers (pick one):
     9→ *     @critical        — must-pass smoke tests; blocks deployment
    10→ *     @standard        — normal regression coverage
    11→ *     @comprehensive   — edge cases, visual, slow scenarios
    12→ *
    13→ *   Features (pick one or more):
    14→ *     @search    @filters    @pagination   @sorting
    15→ *     @views     @detail     @columns      @geo
    16→ *     @blend     @export     @auth         @nav
    17→ *     @responsive
    18→ *
    19→ *   Example test title:
    20→ *     'loads search page and displays results @critical @search'
    21→ */
    22→
    23→/**
    24→ * Navigate to the /search page with the given URL search params.
    25→ *
    26→ * @example
    27→ *   await searchWith(page, { q: 'MIT', state: 'MA' })
    28→ *   // navigates to /search?q=MIT&state=MA
    29→ */
    30→export async function searchWith(
    31→  page: Page,
    32→  params: Record<string, string>,
    33→): Promise<void> {
    34→  const search = new URLSearchParams(params).toString()
    35→  const url = search ? `/search?${search}` : '/search'
    36→  await page.goto(url)
    37→}
    38→
    39→/**
    40→ * Wait for the search results skeleton to clear and results to become visible.
    41→ * Asserts that the loading indicator disappears and at least one result or
    42→ * the "institutions found" / "results" text is visible.
    43→ */
    44→export async function waitForResults(page: Page): Promise<void> {
    45→  // Wait for the loading skeleton to disappear
    46→  await expect(page.getByTestId('results-loading')).toBeHidden({
    47→    timeout: 30_000,
    48→  })
    49→
    50→  // Wait for the results summary text to appear
    51→  // Matches both "Showing 1-20 of 42 institutions found" and "100+ results"
    52→  await expect(
    53→    page.getByText(/institutions found|results/i),
    54→  ).toBeVisible({ timeout: 10_000 })
    55→}
    56→
    57→/**
    58→ * Parse the displayed result count from the results summary text.
    59→ * Handles both "Showing X-Y of Z institutions found" and "100+ results" formats.
    60→ *
    61→ * @returns The total number of results, or 101 for the "100+" display.
    62→ */
    63→export async function getResultCount(page: Page): Promise<number> {
    64→  const summaryLocator = page.getByText(/Showing \d+/i)
    65→  const text = await summaryLocator.textContent()
    66→
    67→  if (!text) {
    68→    throw new Error('Could not find results summary text on page')
    69→  }
    70→
    71→  // Match "100+ results" format
    72→  const plusMatch = text.match(/(\d+)\+\s*results/i)
    73→  if (plusMatch) {
    74→    return Number(plusMatch[1]) + 1
    75→  }
    76→
    77→  // Match "Showing X-Y of Z institutions found" or "Showing X-Y of Z,ZZZ institutions found"
    78→  const ofMatch = text.match(/of\s+([\d,]+)/i)
    79→  if (ofMatch) {
    80→    return Number(ofMatch[1].replace(/,/g, ''))
    81→  }
    82→

{"parentUuid":null,"isSidechain":true,"userType":"external","cwd":"/home/ketan/project/kapow/.claude/worktrees/agent-a5ef5795","sessionId":"671e5b9c-c068-4bab-b9b5-75da76d0514f","version":"2.1.71","gitBranch":"worktree-agent-a5ef5795","agentId":"a5ef5795b44935594","slug":"golden-plotting-crane","type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":"## Task: Write E2E tests for kapow-xll (Authentication & Preferences)

Create `web/e2e/auth-prefs.spec.ts` with Playwright E2E tests for authentication UI states and user preferences.

### Issue Description
End-to-end tests for authentication UI states and user preferences. Tag: `@auth`.

Requires either a test Supabase account or mock auth setup for signed-in state tests.

**Tests needed:**

### Critical (~1 test)
- Unauthenticated state: 'Sign In' button visible in header

### Standard (~2 tests)
- Sign-in dropdown shows 'Continue with Google' option
- (If mock auth available) Signed-in state shows user menu with name/email

### Comprehensive (~1 test)
- Preferences round-trip: save home location → reload page → location persists

### Notes from issue
- Full OAuth flow testing requires either a test account or mock Supabase setup
- Consider using Playwright's route interception to mock auth responses

### Existing patterns to follow

Import from helpers:
```ts
import { test, expect } from '@playwright/test'
import { searchWith, waitForResults } from './helpers'
```

### Tag convention
Each test title ends with tier + feature tags: `@critical @auth`, `@standard @auth`, `@comprehensive @auth`

### Key UI selectors (from actual component code)

**Sign In Menu** (`SignInMenu.tsx`):
- Sign In button: `page.getByRole('button', { name: 'Sign In' })` — visible when user is NOT logged in
- Variant: "accent-outline" (gold border)
- Clicking opens a Mantine Menu dropdown
- Menu item: "Continue with Google" — triggers `supabase.auth.signInWithOAuth({ provider: 'google' })`

**User Menu** (`UserMenu.tsx`) — visible when user IS logged in:
- Avatar element showing user initials or image
- Click opens menu with:
  - User's full name (from `user_metadata.full_name`)
  - User's email
  - Divider
  - "Sign out" menu item (red color)

**Header layout**:
- Desktop (≥768px): Auth buttons in header right side
- Mobile (<768px): Auth components in mobile drawer

**Location Preferences** (for preferences round-trip test):
- Location is stored in localStorage or URL params
- The LocationInput component allows setting ZIP code
- ZIP input: `page.getByLabel('ZIP code')` with "Go" button
- Once set, location appears as "City, State" text
- URL gets `?userLat=...&userLng=...&userState=...`
- On reload, if URL params are present, location is restored

**Important implementation notes**:
1. For the unauthenticated tests, no mock setup needed — just verify the Sign In button and dropdown
2. For the signed-in test, use Playwright's route interception to mock the Supabase auth state. The app reads auth from a Zustand store (`authStore`) that hydrates from Supabase. You can either:
   - Mock the Supabase auth endpoint responses
   - Or skip this test with `test.skip()` and a comment explaining it needs mock auth setup
   - **Recommended**: Skip the signed-in test for now with a clear comment, since mocking Supabase auth state is complex and brittle
3. For preferences round-trip, use ZIP code entry (which triggers a GraphQL query), then reload and verify. Since this needs a running server, the test will work when E2E tests run against the dev server.

### Quality gate
After writing the spec file, run:
```bash
cd web && pnpm build && pnpm lint
```
This must pass with zero errors/warnings.

### Git
Commit with message format:
```
feat: add E2E tests for authentication UI and preferences

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
```

{"parentUuid":"bf44879f-cea1-4ade-97c5-6f6af7035e19","isSidechain":true,"userType":"external","cwd":"/home/ketan/project/kapow/.claude/worktrees/agent-a5ef5795","sessionId":"671e5b9c-c068-4bab-b9b5-75da76d0514f","version":"2.1.71","gitBranch":"worktree-agent-afb4f270","agentId":"a5ef5795b44935594","slug":"golden-plotting-crane","type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01Ex7ctcFkcPnGnQpzojn4Do","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→import { type Page, expect } from '@playwright/test'
     2→
     3→/**
     4→ * Shared E2E test utilities for Kapow.
     5→ *
     6→ * Tagging convention for test titles:
     7→ *
     8→ *   Tiers (pick one):
     9→ *     @critical        — must-pass smoke tests; blocks deployment
    10→ *     @standard        — normal regression coverage
    11→ *     @comprehensive   — edge cases, visual, slow scenarios
    12→ *
    13→ *   Features (pick one or more):
    14→ *     @search    @filters    @pagination   @sorting
    15→ *     @views     @detail     @columns      @geo
    16→ *     @blend     @export     @auth         @nav
    17→ *     @responsive
    18→ *
    19→ *   Example test title:
    20→ *     'loads search page and displays results @critical @search'
    21→ */
    22→
    23→/**
    24→ * Navigate to the /search page with the given URL search params.
    25→ *
    26→ * @example
    27→ *   await searchWith(page, { q: 'MIT', state: 'MA' })
    28→ *   // navigates to /search?q=MIT&state=MA
    29→ */
    30→export async function searchWith(
    31→  page: Page,
    32→  params: Record<string, string>,
    33→): Promise<void> {
    34→  const search = new URLSearchParams(params).toString()
    35→  const url = search ? `/search?${search}` : '/search'
    36→  await page.goto(url)
    37→}
    38→
    39→/**
    40→ * Wait for the search results skeleton to clear and results to become visible.
    41→ * Asserts that the loading indicator disappears and at least one result or
    42→ * the "institutions found" / "results" text is visible.
    43→ */
    44→export async function waitForResults(page: Page): Promise<void> {
    45→  // Wait for the loading skeleton to disappear
    46→  await expect(page.getByTestId('results-loading')).toBeHidden({
    47→    timeout: 30_000,
    48→  })
    49→
    50→  // Wait for the results summary text to appear
    51→  // Matches both "Showing 1-20 of 42 institutions found" and "100+ results"
    52→  await expect(
    53→    page.getByText(/institutions found|results/i),
    54→  ).toBeVisible({ timeout: 10_000 })
    55→}
    56→
    57→/**
    58→ * Parse the displayed result count from the results summary text.
    59→ * Handles both "Showing X-Y of Z institutions found" and "100+ results" formats.
    60→ *
    61→ * @returns The total number of results, or 101 for the "100+" display.
    62→ */
    63→export async function getResultCount(page: Page): Promise<number> {
    64→  const summaryLocator = page.getByText(/Showing \d+/i)
    65→  const text = await summaryLocator.textContent()
    66→
    67→  if (!text) {
    68→    throw new Error('Could not find results summary text on page')
    69→  }
    70→
    71→  // Match "100+ results" format
    72→  const plusMatch = text.match(/(\d+)\+\s*results/i)
    73→  if (plusMatch) {
    74→    return Number(plusMatch[1]) + 1
    75→  }
    76→
    77→  // Match "Showing X-Y of Z institutions found" or "Showing X-Y of Z,ZZZ institutions found"
    78→  const ofMatch = text.match(/of\s+([\d,]+)/i)
    79→  if (ofMatch) {
    80→    return Number(ofMatch[1].replace(/,/g, ''))
    81→  }
    82→
    83→  throw new Error(`Could not parse result count from text: "${text}"`)
    84→}
    85→"}]},"uuid":"3fe8ac5d-e67b-4f50-b04f-29d20a7bc429","timestamp":"2026-03-09T02:09:51.223Z","sourceToolAssistantUUID":"bf44879f-cea1-4ade-97c5-6f6af7035e19"}

End-to-end tests for the table view column picker. Tag: `@columns`.

- Table view shows expected default columns

- Open column picker → uncheck a column → column header disappears from table
- Re-check the column → column reappears

### Comprehensive (~2 tests)
- Column visibility persists across page reload (localStorage `kapow:visible-columns`)


### Critical (~1 test)
- Table view shows expected default columns

### Standard (~2 tests)
- Open column picker → uncheck a column → column header disappears from table
- Re-check the column → column reappears

### Comprehensive (~2 tests)
- Column visibility persists across page reload (localStorage `kapow:visible-columns`)
- Column order changes via picker are reflected in table header order

### Existing patterns to follow

Import from helpers:
```ts
import { test, expect } from '@playwright/test'
import { searchWith, waitForResults } from './helpers'
```

To switch to table view:
```ts
await page.locator('input[value="table"] + label').click()
```

### Tag convention
Each test title ends with tier + feature tags: `@critical @columns`, `@standard @columns`, `@comprehensive @columns`

### Key UI selectors (from actual component code)

**View Toggle**: SegmentedControl with `input[value="table"] + label` to click table view

**Column Picker** (`ColumnPicker.tsx`):
- Trigger button: `page.getByRole('button', { name: 'Columns' })` — only visible in table view
- Opens a Mantine Popover dropdown (width 300px, max-height 500px scrollable)

**Inside the popover:**
- Checkboxes grouped by theme headings (CORE, LOCATION, ACADEMICS, COST, OUTCOMES, RANKINGS)
- Each checkbox has a label with the column display name (e.g., "City", "State", "Enrollment", etc.)
- The "Name" checkbox is always checked and disabled (pinned column)
- Use: `popover.getByRole('checkbox', { name: 'City' })` to find a checkbox
- Check/uncheck: `checkbox.click()` or `checkbox.check()` / `checkbox.uncheck()`

**Column Order section** (below checkboxes, after divider):
- Heading: "Column Order"
- Each reorderable item: `data-testid="column-order-item-{fieldName}"` (e.g., `column-order-item-city`)
- Up arrow button: `aria-label="Move {label} up"`
- Down arrow button: `aria-label="Move {label} down"`

**Table headers**:
- The results table `<table>` has `<th>` elements
- Sortable headers have `data-testid="sort-{fieldName}"` (e.g., `sort-name`, `sort-city`)
- Theme group spanning headers: `data-testid="theme-group-{THEME}"` (e.g., `theme-group-COST`)

**localStorage keys**:
- `kapow:visible-columns` — JSON array of visible column field names
- `kapow:column-order` — JSON array of column order

**Default visible columns**: The default set includes Name (always), City, State, and a few others. Check what's visible by default in table view without any localStorage set.

**Important notes**:
- First navigate to `/search` and wait for results, then switch to table view
- The column picker popover closes when clicking outside — keep interactions within it
- After toggling a column, the table re-renders; wait for the table to be visible
- For column order test, look at the actual `<th>` elements' text content order in the table

### Quality gate
After writing the spec file, run:
```bash
cd web && pnpm build && pnpm lint
```
This must pass with zero errors/warnings.

### Git
Commit with message format:
```
feat: add E2E tests for table column customization

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
```

{"parentUuid":"dd159cea-bbd6-4837-abb1-4de20833ad94","isSidechain":true,"userType":"external","cwd":"/home/ketan/project/kapow/.claude/worktrees/agent-a36d7ab2","sessionId":"671e5b9c-c068-4bab-b9b5-75da76d0514f","version":"2.1.71","gitBranch":"worktree-agent-a6b26db5","agentId":"a36d7ab2e667d68a0","slug":"golden-plotting-crane","type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_018c62HGsPafRT2nZTf9a5iV","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→import { useRef, useState } from 'react'
     2→import {
     3→  ActionIcon,
     4→  Box,
     5→  Button,
     6→  Checkbox,
     7→  Divider,
     8→  Group,
     9→  Popover,
    10→  Stack,
    11→  Text,
    12→} from '@mantine/core'
    13→import { ArrowDown, ArrowUp, Columns, GripVertical } from 'lucide-react'
    14→
    15→import { outputFieldsByTheme } from './helpers'
    16→import type { SearchOutputField } from './types'
    17→
    18→interface ColumnPickerProps {
    19→  outputFields: SearchOutputField[]
    20→  visibleColumns: Set<string>
    21→  onToggleColumn: (fieldName: string) => void
    22→  columnOrder: string[]
    23→  onReorderColumn: (fieldName: string, direction: 'up' | 'down') => void
    24→  onSetColumnOrder: (order: string[]) => void
    25→}
    26→
    27→export function ColumnPicker({
    28→  outputFields,
    29→  visibleColumns,
    30→  onToggleColumn,
    31→  columnOrder,
    32→  onReorderColumn,
    33→  onSetColumnOrder,
    34→}: ColumnPickerProps) {
    35→  const visibleFields = outputFields.filter((f) => f.visibility !== 'HIDDEN')
    36→  const groups = outputFieldsByTheme(visibleFields)
    37→
    38→  // Build ordered list of visible columns (excluding pinned "name") for the reorder section
    39→  const fieldsByName = new Map(outputFields.map((f) => [f.name, f]))
    40→  const orderedVisible = columnOrder.filter(
    41→    (name) => name !== 'name' && visibleColumns.has(name) && fieldsByName.has(name),
    42→  )
    43→
    44→  // Drag state
    45→  const dragItem = useRef<number | null>(null)
    46→  const dragOverItem = useRef<number | null>(null)
    47→  const [draggingIdx, setDraggingIdx] = useState<number | null>(null)
    48→  const [dragOverIdx, setDragOverIdx] = useState<number | null>(null)
    49→
    50→  function handleDragStart(idx: number) {
    51→    dragItem.current = idx
    52→    setDraggingIdx(idx)
    53→  }
    54→
    55→  function handleDragEnter(idx: number) {
    56→    dragOverItem.current = idx
    57→    setDragOverIdx(idx)
    58→  }
    59→
    60→  function handleDragEnd() {
    61→    if (dragItem.current !== null && dragOverItem.current !== null && dragItem.current !== dragOverItem.current) {
    62→      // Reorder orderedVisible, then map back to full columnOrder
    63→      const reordered = [...orderedVisible]
    64→      const [removed] = reordered.splice(dragItem.current, 1)
    65→      reordered.splice(dragOverItem.current, 0, removed)
    66→
    67→      // Rebuild full column order: preserve items not in orderedVisible at their positions,
    68→      // replace orderedVisible items with the reordered list
    69→      const orderedVisibleSet = new Set(orderedVisible)
    70→      const newOrder: string[] = []
    71→      let reorderIdx = 0
    72→      for (const name of columnOrder) {
    73→        if (orderedVisibleSet.has(name)) {
    74→          newOrder.push(reordered[reorderIdx++])
    75→        } else {
    76→          newOrder.push(name)
    77→        }
    78→      }
    79→      onSetColumnOrder(newOrder)
    80→    }
    81→    dragItem.current = null
    82→    dragOverItem.current = null
    83→    setDraggingIdx(null)
    84→    setDragOverIdx(null)
    85→  }
    86→
    87→  return (
    88→    <Popover width={300} position="bottom-end" shadow="md">
    89→      <Popover.Target>
    90→        <Button variant="default" leftSection={<Columns size={16} />}>
    91→          Columns
    92→        </Button>
    93→      </Popover.Target>
    94→      <Popover.Dropdown>
    95→        <Box style={{ maxHeight: 500, overflowY: 'auto' }}>
    96→          <Stack gap="md">
    97→            {groups.map((group) => (
    98→              <Box key={group.theme}>
    99→                <Text fw={600} fz="sm" mb="xs">
   100→                  {group.label}
   101→                </Text>
   102→                <Stack gap="xs">
   103→                  {group.fields.map((field) => {
   104→                    const isName = field.name === 'name'
   105→                    return (
   106→                      <Group key={field.name} gap="xs">
   107→                        <Checkbox
   108→                          label={field.label}
   109→                          checked={isName || visibleColumns.has(field.name)}
   110→                          disabled={isName}
   111→                          onChange={() => onToggleColumn(field.name)}
   112→                          size="sm"
   113→                        />
   114→                      </Group>
   115→                    )
   116→                  })}
   117→                </Stack>
   118→              </Box>
   119→            ))}
   120→
   121→            {orderedVisible.length > 0 && (
   122→              <>
   123→                <Divider />
   124→                <Box>
   125→                  <Text fw={600} fz="sm" mb="xs">
   126→                    Column Order
   127→                  </Text>
   128→                  <Stack gap={4}>
   129→                    {orderedVisible.map((name, idx) => {
   130→                      const field = fieldsByName.get(name)

{"parentUuid":null,"isSidechain":true,"userType":"external","cwd":"/home/ketan/project/kapow/.claude/worktrees/agent-afb4f270","sessionId":"671e5b9c-c068-4bab-b9b5-75da76d0514f","version":"2.1.71","gitBranch":"worktree-agent-a6b26db5","agentId":"afb4f270a6c258146","slug":"golden-plotting-crane","type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":"## Task: Write E2E tests for kapow-9iu (Navigation, Deep Links & Responsive)

Create `web/e2e/nav-responsive.spec.ts` with Playwright E2E tests for navigation, URL deep linking, and responsive layouts.

### Issue Description
End-to-end tests for navigation, URL deep linking, and responsive layouts. Tags: `@nav`, `@responsive`.

**Tests needed:**

### Critical (~1 test)
- Navigate directly to `/search?state=CA` → state filter pre-populated, matching results shown

### Standard (~3 tests)
- Header nav: Home → About → Home works via nav links
- Logo click returns to home from any page
- Mobile viewport (375px): hamburger menu appears, desktop nav hidden

### Comprehensive (~3 tests)
- Mobile: filter collapse 'Show/Hide Filters' toggle works on search page
- Tablet (768px): layout adapts correctly (collapsible filters above results)
- All filters round-trip through URL: set filters → copy URL → navigate to URL → same filter state and results

### Existing patterns to follow

Import from helpers:
```ts
import { test, expect } from '@playwright/test'
import { searchWith, waitForResults, getResultCount } from './helpers'
```

### Tag convention
Each test title ends with tier + feature tags: `@critical @nav`, `@standard @nav`, `@standard @responsive`, `@comprehensive @responsive`, etc.

### Key UI selectors (from actual component code)

**Header** (`Header.tsx`):
- Logo link: `<a>` or `<Link>` wrapping GraduationCap icon + "College Power Search" text, links to "/"
  - Find via: `page.getByRole('link', { name: /College Power Search/i })` or `page.locator('a[href="/"]').filter({ hasText: 'College Power Search' })`
- Desktop nav links (visible from `md` breakpoint = 768px+):
  - Home: `page.getByRole('link', { name: 'Home' })` (NavLink to "/")
  - About: `page.getByRole('link', { name: 'About' })` (NavLink to "/about")
- Mobile burger: `page.getByRole('button', { name: 'Toggle navigation' })` or `button[aria-label="Toggle navigation"]`
  - Only visible below `md` breakpoint (< 768px)
- Mobile drawer: Mantine Drawer with title "Navigation", contains Home + About links

**Important Mantine breakpoints**:
- `md` = 768px — below this, desktop nav hides and burger appears
- Default viewport in Playwright is 1280x720, so desktop nav is visible by default

**Deep linking / URL state**:
- Filters serialize to URL: `?state=CA`, `?q=MIT`, `?enrollment=1000,5000`, etc.
- State filter in sidebar: `page.locator('label', { has: page.getByText('State') }).locator('select')` — check its value
- Name filter: `page.getByRole('textbox', { name: 'Name' })`

**Filter sidebar on mobile**:
- On mobile, the filter sidebar collapses. Look for a "Show Filters" / "Hide Filters" toggle button.
- Or it may use a "Filters" button that opens a drawer/collapse panel.
- Use `page.getByRole('button', { name: /Filters|Show Filters|Hide Filters/i })` to find it.

**About page**: Route is `/about` — verify it has heading content

### Responsive testing approach
Use `page.setViewportSize({ width: 375, height: 812 })` for mobile and `page.setViewportSize({ width: 768, height: 1024 })` for tablet. Reset to default for desktop tests if needed.

### Quality gate
After writing the spec file, run:
```bash
cd web && pnpm build && pnpm lint
```
This must pass with zero errors/warnings.

### Git
Commit with message format:
```
feat: add E2E tests for navigation, deep links, and responsive layouts

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
```

{"parentUuid":"92dba5dd-78fd-41a4-9db2-10fc527eeca3","isSidechain":true,"userType":"external","cwd":"/home/ketan/project/kapow/.claude/worktrees/agent-afb4f270","sessionId":"671e5b9c-c068-4bab-b9b5-75da76d0514f","version":"2.1.71","gitBranch":"worktree-agent-ae41096b","agentId":"afb4f270a6c258146","slug":"golden-plotting-crane","type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01CMZjDVX63KyGgBMaVAW83j","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→import { type Page, expect } from '@playwright/test'
     2→
     3→/**
     4→ * Shared E2E test utilities for Kapow.
     5→ *
     6→ * Tagging convention for test titles:
     7→ *
     8→ *   Tiers (pick one):
     9→ *     @critical        — must-pass smoke tests; blocks deployment
    10→ *     @standard        — normal regression coverage
    11→ *     @comprehensive   — edge cases, visual, slow scenarios
    12→ *
    13→ *   Features (pick one or more):
    14→ *     @search    @filters    @pagination   @sorting
    15→ *     @views     @detail     @columns      @geo
    16→ *     @blend     @export     @auth         @nav
    17→ *     @responsive
    18→ *
    19→ *   Example test title:
    20→ *     'loads search page and displays results @critical @search'
    21→ */
    22→
    23→/**
    24→ * Navigate to the /search page with the given URL search params.
    25→ *
    26→ * @example
    27→ *   await searchWith(page, { q: 'MIT', state: 'MA' })
    28→ *   // navigates to /search?q=MIT&state=MA
    29→ */
    30→export async function searchWith(
    31→  page: Page,
    32→  params: Record<string, string>,
    33→): Promise<void> {
    34→  const search = new URLSearchParams(params).toString()
    35→  const url = search ? `/search?${search}` : '/search'
    36→  await page.goto(url)
    37→}
    38→
    39→/**
    40→ * Wait for the search results skeleton to clear and results to become visible.
    41→ * Asserts that the loading indicator disappears and at least one result or
    42→ * the "institutions found" / "results" text is visible.
    43→ */
    44→export async function waitForResults(page: Page): Promise<void> {
    45→  // Wait for the loading skeleton to disappear
    46→  await expect(page.getByTestId('results-loading')).toBeHidden({
    47→    timeout: 30_000,
    48→  })
    49→
    50→  // Wait for the results summary text to appear
    51→  // Matches both "Showing 1-20 of 42 institutions found" and "100+ results"
    52→  await expect(
    53→    page.getByText(/institutions found|results/i),
    54→  ).toBeVisible({ timeout: 10_000 })
    55→}
    56→
    57→/**
    58→ * Parse the displayed result count from the results summary text.
    59→ * Handles both "Showing X-Y of Z institutions found" and "100+ results" formats.
    60→ *
    61→ * @returns The total number of results, or 101 for the "100+" display.
    62→ */
    63→export async function getResultCount(page: Page): Promise<number> {
    64→  const summaryLocator = page.getByText(/Showing \d+/i)
    65→  const text = await summaryLocator.textContent()
    66→
    67→  if (!text) {
    68→    throw new Error('Could not find results summary text on page')
    69→  }
    70→
    71→  // Match "100+ results" format
    72→  const plusMatch = text.match(/(\d+)\+\s*results/i)
    73→  if (plusMatch) {
    74→    return Number(plusMatch[1]) + 1
    75→  }
    76→
    77→  // Match "Showing X-Y of Z institutions found" or "Showing X-Y of Z,ZZZ institutions found"
    78→  const ofMatch = text.match(/of\s+([\d,]+)/i)
    79→  if (ofMatch) {
    80→    return Number(ofMatch[1].replace(/,/g, ''))
    81→  }
    82→
    83→  throw new Error(`Could not parse result count from text: "${text}"`)
    84→}
    85→"}]},"uuid":"e9b99d6e-db97-41a9-9206-b4cb2d7eea6f","timestamp":"2026-03-09T02:09:31.057Z","sourceToolAssistantUUID":"92dba5dd-78fd-41a4-9db2-10fc527eeca3"}

content.text:
All done. Regarding your question about agents not showing in tmux panes — Claude Code's `Agent` tool runs subagents as in-process child conversations, not as separate terminal processes. They don't spawn tmux panes or separate shells. The worktree isolation gives them a separate git working directory, but execution happens within the same Claude Code process. There's no built-in tmux integration for agent visualization.

**Summary of what was completed:**

All 5 in-progress issues investigated, found to have zero completed work (empty branches), and completed:

| Issue | File | Tests |
|---|---|---|
| kapow-25l | `blend-export.spec.ts` | 7 tests (blend sliders, reset, CSV/PDF export, URL params, percentages) |
| kapow-7at | `geo-features.spec.ts` | 6 tests (ZIP resolve, distance display, sort-by-distance, sample search, invalid ZIP, distance validation) |
| kapow-ce0 | `table-columns.spec.ts` | 5 tests (default columns, toggle visibility, persistence via localStorage, column order) |
| kapow-9iu | `nav-responsive.spec.ts` | 7 tests (deep links, nav links, logo, mobile burger, filter toggle, tablet layout, URL round-trip) |
| kapow-xll | `auth-prefs.spec.ts` | 4 tests (sign-in button, Google option, skipped signed-in test, location persistence) |

**29 total E2E tests** added. Build and lint pass. All 5 issues closed.
